name: Technology & equipment
description: Semiconductor/computer/electronics output and prices plus business equipment & software investment.

series.note: Direct physical-output gauge of US semiconductor and electronic component production, the cleanest real-activity signal for the chip supply chain.
Broad output index for computers and electronic products, capturing aggregate tech-hardware manufacturing momentum.
NAICS-334 production index tracks the full computer-and-electronics manufacturing complex for demand-trend analysis.
Tracks selling-price trends for semiconductors and components, a direct read on chip pricing power and margins.
Narrow PPI for semiconductor devices isolates chip price deflation/inflation that drives memory and logic margins.
Measures computer/server selling prices, informing hardware ASP trends and OEM pricing power.
Tracks pricing for data processing, hosting and cloud-adjacent services, a margin and demand signal for cloud/data infrastructure.
GDP-account measure of business spending on computers/IT equipment, a direct demand line for tech hardware.
GDP-component capturing business investment in software, the cleanest macro line for enterprise/IP software demand.
Total business equipment investment frames the capex cycle that drives hardware and equipment vendors.
Pricing for test-and-measurement instruments, relevant to electronics test/inspection equipment margins.
Headcount in IT services/computer systems design is a demand and labor-cost signal for the tech-services sector.
Consumer electronics retail demand signal for PCs, phones, and devices at the channel level.
Captures external demand, important because semiconductors and hardware are heavily export-dependent.
